creator: ggg_mail@inbox.ru
text:
Updated to 1.76, and have just experrienced the same crash again.

Maybe I should not blame transmission-gtk2 port but transmission-web of  
this bug. The structure of this application is not very clear to me, and  
there's no explanation on the official site on what every component of it  
does. On my system the following ports related to transmission are  
installed:

$ ls -ld /var/db/pkg/transmission-*

drwxr-xr-x  2 root  wheel  512  8 ÑÎ× 01:40 /var/db/pkg/transmission-1.76
drwxr-xr-x  2 root  wheel  512 24 ÄÅË 12:41  
/var/db/pkg/transmission-cli-1.76
drwxr-xr-x  2 root  wheel  512 24 ÄÅË 12:41  
/var/db/pkg/transmission-daemon-1.76
drwxr-xr-x  2 root  wheel  512  8 ÑÎ× 01:40  
/var/db/pkg/transmission-gtk2-1.76
drwxr-xr-x  2 root  wheel  512 24 ÄÅË 12:41  
/var/db/pkg/transmission-web-1.76

And I start GTK interface and then set up web-interface from it, so it's   
unclear for me who is in charge - GTK, Web or even daemon part  of  
Transmission.

On Tue, 26 Jan 2010 05:29:18 +0300, Jeremy Messenger <mezz7@cox.net> wrote:

> You will have to update all of your installed ports. The 1.73 is too  
> old. We have 1.76 in ports tree. If you still have this problem, you  
> will have to recompile transmission with the debug and run it in gdb to  
> collect the backtraces.
>
> I can't reproduce this problem.
>
> Cheers,
> Mezz
>
>
